title: Israeli Strikes Kill Five in Gaza — Three in Jabalia, Two in Khan Younis
detail: Israeli airstrikes killed at least five Palestinians in separate attacks across Gaza on March 31, 2026. An airstrike in Jabalia, in northern Gaza, killed at least three people; a second strike in Khan Younis in the south killed two others. Casualties were transferred to nearby hospitals. The IDF confirmed operations against individuals it identified as militants. Palestinian medics and the Gaza Government Media Office recorded both incidents. The strikes followed a pattern of continued Israeli targeted operations under the ceasefire framework, which Israel says excludes operations against active combatants and Hamas security infrastructure. Gaza's Palestine Observatory reported that 138 Palestinians were killed or wounded over the preceding 24-hour period as Israeli operations intensified in multiple governorates despite the five-month-old ceasefire.

title: Gaza Death Toll Reaches 72,285 Since October 2023; 680+ Killed During Ceasefire
detail: The cumulative Palestinian death toll in Gaza since October 7, 2023 reached 72,285 as of March 31, 2026, according to WAFA and the Gaza Ministry of Health. The figure includes mostly women and children, per UN OCHA breakdowns. More than 680 Palestinians have been killed by Israeli forces since the US-brokered ceasefire came into effect in October 2025. The ceasefire, which ended the full-scale ground offensive, has failed to halt Israeli targeted operations that continue on a near-daily basis. Human rights organizations including Al-Haq and Amnesty International said March 31 that Israel is obligated under the ceasefire agreement to fully cease hostilities, not just halt ground operations. The Gaza Government Media Office recorded 2,073 Israeli ceasefire violations between October 10, 2025 and March 18, 2026.

title: Hezbollah Fires Rocket Barrage at Northern Israel Causing Three Light Injuries; IDF Strikes Launchers
detail: Hezbollah fired a rocket barrage at northern Israel on March 31, 2026, causing three light injuries. The IDF confirmed it struck Hezbollah launch sites in southern Lebanon in response. The attack came on the same day Israeli airstrikes killed five Palestinians in Gaza and Houthis prepared to resume missile attacks on Israel, underlining the multi-front pressure on Israel as the broader Iran-led conflict expanded in late March 2026. The Lebanon ceasefire brokered by US Special Envoy Amos Hochstein in November 2024 has been under increasing strain as Hezbollah conducted periodic fire and Israel conducted occasional counter-strikes. The March 31 barrage represented a clear escalation signal, coordinated with the Houthi resumption of attacks on March 30 and the Axis of Resistance's stated solidarity campaign. No life-threatening injuries were reported; Israeli emergency services treated three lightly wounded persons in the affected communities.
